plan_type_rationale: Multiple independent improvement areas each with its own verification gate. Sequential delivery; each phase ships independently.

phases.name: Source-Headers Coverage Flood
phases.goal: Run --source-headers on CODEREF-CORE to populate the 2382 missing element headers and raise RAG coverage from 15% to ~85%+

tasks.description: Run populate-coderef --source-headers on CODEREF-CORE project root
tasks.success: CLI exits 0; .coderef/index.json shows headerStatus:present for majority of elements

tasks.description: Verify RAG-indexable element count — run rag-index and compare before/after coverage numbers
tasks.success: Coverage rises from ~15% to >=80%

tasks.description: Commit updated .coderef/ artifacts
tasks.success: Git commit clean; message: 'feat(source-headers): populate element headers for CODEREF-CORE — RAG coverage <before>% → <after>%'

acceptance_criteria: headerStatus:missing count drops from 2382 to <200
RAG index coverage >= 80%
.coderef/ snapshot committed

phases.name: Tree-Sitter Field Verification
phases.goal: Confirm that returnType, decorators, docstring, and complexity fields now populate in scanner output for TS/JS/Go/Rust/Java/C++ after tree-sitter promotion (WO-SCANNER-AST-COVERAGE-001)

tasks.description: Read src/scanner/tree-sitter-scanner.ts — check field extraction for each language grammar (returnType, decorators, docstring, complexity)
tasks.success: Know which fields each grammar currently extracts

tasks.description: Run coderef-scan on a known TS/Go/Rust/Java/C++ fixture and inspect output for enrichment fields
tasks.success: Audit table: per-language, per-field populated/missing

tasks.description: Fix any grammar extractors that are confirmed missing fields (only if gaps found in task above)
tasks.success: All 6 languages populate returnType, decorators, docstring where language supports them

tasks.description: Run vitest — 0 regressions
tasks.success: npx vitest --run exits 0

tasks.description: Commit enrichment field fixes if any were made (skip if no gaps found)
tasks.success: Commit message: 'fix(scanner): fill enrichment field gaps in tree-sitter grammars (IMP-CORE-052 followup)'

acceptance_criteria: returnType populated for TS/JS function elements
decorators populated for TS class/method elements where present
complexity populated for all 6 languages
0 vitest regressions

phases.name: Export Edges
phases.goal: Emit export-type edges in graph-builder.ts so the graph reflects which elements are exported from each file

tasks.description: Read src/graph/graph-builder.ts — find where EdgeRelationship types are constructed; confirm export type is defined but skipped
tasks.success: Exact line(s) where export edges are suppressed identified

tasks.description: Wire export edge emission: for each element with exported=true, emit EdgeRelationship{type:'export', from:file, to:element}
tasks.success: Export edges appear in graph output for known exported elements

tasks.description: Run vitest — 0 regressions; spot-check graph output for a known TS file with exports
tasks.success: npx vitest --run exits 0; graph.json contains export edges

tasks.description: Commit export edge wiring
tasks.success: Commit message: 'feat(graph): emit export edges for exported elements'

acceptance_criteria: graph.json contains EdgeRelationship entries with type='export'
Only elements with exported=true receive export edges
0 vitest regressions

phases.name: Fix what-imports vs what-depends-on Divergence
phases.goal: Make what-imports and what-depends-on return different, correct results — currently both call getDependencies() and return identical output

tasks.description: Read src/cli/coderef-query.ts — find what-imports and what-depends-on handler implementations; confirm they both call getDependencies()
tasks.success: Root cause confirmed: identical implementations

tasks.description: Fix what-imports to return import-graph edges (what files does this file import); fix what-depends-on to return dependency-graph edges (what files depend on this file's exports)
tasks.success: what-imports returns outbound imports; what-depends-on returns inbound dependents

tasks.description: Run vitest + manual spot-check: what-imports <file> and what-depends-on <file> return different results for a file with known importers
tasks.success: Results differ; both are semantically correct

tasks.description: Commit what-imports/what-depends-on divergence fix
tasks.success: Commit message: 'fix(query): diverge what-imports (outbound) from what-depends-on (inbound)'

acceptance_criteria: what-imports returns files this module imports from
what-depends-on returns files that import this module
The two commands return different result sets for any non-trivial file
0 vitest regressions

phases.name: rag-search --constraint Filter
phases.goal: Implement the deferred --constraint filter for rag-search CLI (DR-PHASE-7-D)

tasks.description: Read src/cli/rag-search.ts — find where --constraint was deferred; understand current filter chain
tasks.success: Know where to inject constraint filtering

tasks.description: Add --constraint <type:value> argument: filter RAG results by element type, file pattern, or tag before returning
tasks.success: --constraint type:function filters to function elements only; --constraint file:src/scanner/* filters to scanner files

tasks.description: Run vitest + manual: rag-search 'scanner element' --constraint type:function returns only function results
tasks.success: Constraint filtering works; 0 regressions

tasks.description: Commit --constraint filter implementation
tasks.success: Commit message: 'feat(rag-search): implement --constraint filter (closes DR-PHASE-7-D)'

acceptance_criteria: --constraint type:<value> filters results to matching element types
--constraint file:<glob> filters results to matching file paths
Unrecognized constraint key returns helpful error
0 vitest regressions

phases.name: Graph Visualization Fields
phases.goal: Populate nodePositions, nodeColors, edgeWeights, and layoutHints fields in graph output (currently defined but never set)

tasks.description: Read src/graph/graph-builder.ts — locate nodePositions, nodeColors, edgeWeights, layoutHints field declarations; confirm none are populated
tasks.success: Know which fields need wiring and what data sources to use

tasks.description: Populate visualization fields: nodeColors by element type, edgeWeights by call frequency, layoutHints from file directory depth
tasks.success: graph.json output contains non-empty visualization fields for a real project scan

tasks.description: Run vitest — 0 regressions; verify graph.json has visualization fields populated
tasks.success: npx vitest --run exits 0; graph.json.nodeColors is non-empty

tasks.description: Commit visualization field population
tasks.success: Commit message: 'feat(graph): populate visualization metadata fields (nodeColors, edgeWeights, layoutHints)'

acceptance_criteria: graph.json.nodeColors maps element type to color token
graph.json.edgeWeights reflects call frequency
graph.json.layoutHints provides depth hints from directory structure
0 vitest regressions

phases.name: ai-prompt-generator Smart Truncation
phases.goal: Replace hard token-count truncation in ai-prompt-generator.ts with structure-preserving truncation that keeps signatures and drops bodies when needed

tasks.description: Read src/analyzer/ai-prompt-generator.ts — find TODO for smart truncation; understand current truncation strategy
tasks.success: Know exact truncation point and what structure is lost

tasks.description: Implement structure-preserving truncation: keep function/class signatures + first-line bodies, drop full implementations when over token budget; append truncation marker
tasks.success: Truncated output preserves all top-level signatures; bodies drop gracefully

tasks.description: Run vitest — 0 regressions; spot-check truncated output for a large TS file
tasks.success: Signatures visible; no runaway token consumption

tasks.description: Commit smart truncation implementation
tasks.success: Commit message: 'feat(ai-prompt-generator): structure-preserving smart truncation'

acceptance_criteria: Truncated output always includes all function/class signatures
Token budget is respected (no over-budget outputs)
Truncation marker present in output when truncation occurred
0 vitest regressions
